text:
<think>I want to begin by clarifying that both sequences are of the same length and involve a fair coin, so I should address the probability of each specific sequence occurring.</think>
Both HHHHH and HTTHT are specific sequences of five coin tosses with a fair coin.
<think>Now I should explain how to calculate the probability of a particular sequence occurring when tossing a fair coin multiple times.</think>
The probability of any particular sequence of five coin tosses is (1/2)^5, which equals 1/32.
<think>I should now explicitly state that both sequences have the same probability, to directly answer the question.</think>
Therefore, both HHHHH and HTTHT are equally likely to occur, each with a probability of 1/32.
<think>To conclude, I may want to briefly explain why people sometimes mistakenly think one sequence is less likely than the other, to address potential confusion.</think>
Although HHHHH may seem less likely because it appears more "ordered," each specific sequence of five fair coin tosses is equally probable.
